text.title: Offload before summarize
text.summary: Write large tool results to a filesystem and pass back paths; only summarize when nothing left to offload AND context is past 85%.

rule.statement: Any tool whose output exceeds ~2000 tokens MUST write the full output to the agent's filesystem and return only a path plus a short snippet (first ~200 tokens or a structured digest). Summarization of conversation history runs only after context utilization crosses 85% AND no offloadable blobs remain in context.
rule.rationale: File offload is lossless and reversible — the agent can re-read the original at any precision later. Summarization is lossy and one-way; doing it first throws away information the agent might need to recover.

section.title: Why two thresholds
section.p: Two thresholds keep behaviour predictable across short and long runs:

list.item: Per-tool-result threshold (~2000 tokens) — applies on every tool call, prevents single oversized blobs from dominating the window.
Conversation threshold (~85% of context window) — applies once per turn, triggers summarization only when offload alone is no longer enough.

example.note: Result envelope is small and uniform; agent can choose to re-read.

example.code: def web_search(query: str) -> str: return provider.search(query) # 30k tokens straight into context
example.note: One call eats a quarter of the window; chains of these force premature summarization.

antipattern.description: Summarizing the conversation at the first sign of pressure (50-60% utilization) instead of offloading the next tool result.
antipattern.reason: Lossy compression applied while reversible space-savings are still available — the agent loses recoverable facts to save tokens it didn't need to save yet.

section.reference: Anatomy of an Agent Harness — Daily Dose of DS.
